inline_comments.message: no reason for change this line

since you throw now dataspaceNotFoundException, you can use current dataspaceRepository.get ...
and no need to replicate code
why this removal?, I dont see reason to change this
data existence validation is not a responsibility of repository.
agreed, lets leave the exception generation in the repository class

1) please see discussion in my commit on exception handling, Claudio and I prefer specialized constructors.

2) what is the conflict here exactly? Please note different anchors can have the same schema set. If the schemaSet does not exists I think we should use a CpsNotFoundException

as before,
I think we need to prioritize exception handling improvement commit here: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/116230

sorry Ruslan, I have looked at this but I think it is the best place to put it.
depending on the need you choose a the find method or the get method. the getMethod will thrown an exception, it will be consistent no matter what usecase/ IF we dont do it here we have to do it in many oterh places and 'hope' future developers will use a common approach...

not sure what you mean with conflict?! I dont think this is an error scenario! If it is you are using the wrong terminology
I see now, I think you mean anchor already associated!
So the scenario is associating with an anchor that is already associated with a different set, correct? Please update your terminology e.g. for the constant i would use ALREADY_ASSCOIATED_ANCHOR
I always like to see positive tets scenario first

the schema set name is irrelevant, it is not allowed to have multiple anchors with the same in name in one dataspace (even if they have different schema sets)

all code related to this whole senario needs to be removed
yep, this is the correct approach for testing this

it's not a responsibility of repository.
it's over-complicated if you follow the rules on repository extension
https://docs.spring.io/spring-data/jpa/docs/current/reference/html/#repositories.custom-implementations
keep consistency and follow similar approach used for other cases e.g. schemaSetRepository.getByDataspaceAndName
I think it would be good with large commits to add more details on a high level what has changed, This can grow as patches are added.
